title: Schlumbergera microsphaerica
taxonomy: Schlumbergera microsphaerica is classified within the family of Cactaceae and the genus of Schlumbergera. It belongs to the order Caryophyllales and the class of Magnoliopsida. The kingdom it falls under is Plantae. Its family, Cactaceae, is further divided into several subfamilies, and Schlumbergera microsphaerica falls under the subfamily of Cactoideae. It is also sometimes classified under the subfamily of Epiphyllioideae.

intro: Schlumbergera microsphaerica, also known as the Christmas cactus (not to be confused with the Easter cactus or Thanksgiving cactus), is a species of flowering plant in the family Cactaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of respiratory infections, wounds, and other conditions. The bioactive constituents of Schlumbergera microsphaerica include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Schlumbergera microsphaerica is commonly used to prepare infusions, tinctures, and salves, which are traditionally used to treat a range of health issues, including colds, fever, and skin irritations.
